# Limitations and Next Steps

## Limitations

### 1. Secondary research only

This pack is based on official documentation, academic studies, and industry reports. It does not yet include your own analytics.

### 2. Platform docs are incomplete by design

Platforms disclose broad ranking signals, not exact weights. Therefore, the strategy must be experimental.

### 3. Academic research is adjacent

Many studies are not directly about TikTok/Reels/Shorts. They provide mechanisms: attention, comprehension, emotion, story, diffusion, and persuasion.

### 4. Grey sources are benchmark data, not laws

Industry benchmarks are useful for hypotheses but are not automatically true for your niche.

### 5. Metrics vary by platform

The same creative variable can affect different signals across TikTok, Reels, and Shorts.

## Next steps

## Phase 1: Build creative genome

```text
Collect 100–300 videos from the target niche.
Tag first frame, hook, text, story, proof, CTA, length, topic, and outcome.
Identify recurring mechanisms.
```

## Phase 2: Run controlled tests

Start with the highest-leverage variables:

```text
first frame
hook
text/captions
first payoff timing
proof
CTA
platform-native packaging
```

## Phase 3: Build dashboards

Track per post:

```text
shown / impressions
chose-to-view / early retention
average watch time
completion
saves
sends/shares
comments
follows/subscribers
search traffic
24h / 72h / 7d / 30d curve
```

## Phase 4: Scale mechanisms

Do not scale a single video. Scale the reason it worked.

```text
first-frame mechanism
hook mechanism
story mechanism
save mechanism
send mechanism
follow mechanism
search mechanism
```

## Phase 5: Build content operating system

Recommended recurring series:

```text
Hook Lab
Caption Lab
Retention Clinic
First-Frame Lab
Algorithm Autopsy
Save/Send Teardown
Search vs Feed Tests
```

## Final operating rule

```text
A video is not a post. It is an experiment.
A winning video is not a lucky event. It is evidence of a mechanism.
The job is to find mechanisms and scale them.
```
